title: Chicken Fricassée

overview.description: Tender chicken gently braised with mushrooms in a light, creamy French sauce.

method: Season the chicken thighs with salt and black pepper.
Melt the butter in a large heavy-based casserole over a medium heat.
Add the chicken and cook gently until lightly coloured but not deeply browned.
Remove the chicken from the casserole.
Add the onion and carrots and cook until softened.
Add the garlic and cook for 1 minute.
Add the mushrooms and cook until softened.
Sprinkle over the flour and stir well.
Cook for 1 minute.
Gradually add the white wine, stirring constantly.
Gradually add the chicken stock, stirring until smooth.
Add the bay leaves and thyme.
Return the chicken to the casserole.
Bring to a gentle simmer.
Cover and cook for 35–45 minutes until the chicken is tender and cooked through.
Remove the chicken from the sauce and keep warm.
Remove the bay leaves and thyme stems.
Stir in the cream and lemon juice.
Simmer gently for a few minutes until the sauce thickens slightly.
Return the chicken to the sauce.
Taste and adjust the seasoning.
Scatter with fresh parsley before serving.

learning.did-you-know: The word fricassée refers to a method of cooking in which meat is cut into pieces, gently cooked and served in a light sauce. It sits somewhere between sautéing and braising.
learning.what-you-will-learn: How to create a smooth sauce using a flour-based roux, gently braise chicken and finish a creamy sauce with acidity.

notes.why-its-in-the-chapman-kitchen: A gentle French chicken dish that teaches the difference between a dark, heavily browned braise and a lighter sauce-based preparation.
chapman-tips: Don't brown the chicken too aggressively. The sauce should remain pale and delicate.
Add the flour before the liquid so it cooks properly and thickens the sauce smoothly.
Add the stock gradually while stirring to avoid lumps.
The lemon juice is important: it stops the cream sauce from becoming heavy.
If the sauce becomes too thick, loosen it with a little extra chicken stock.
notes.batch-cooking: Cool completely before freezing in portions. The cream sauce may separate slightly when reheated, so reheat gently and stir well.
notes.leftovers: Remove the chicken from the bones and use the meat and sauce in a pie, with pasta or over rice.
